reviews.review_description: This is probably one of the scariest backrooms vr games I’ve seen. There’s basically no jumpscares which just adds to the atmosphere and increases paranoia. You think that anything can be around any corner and it is terrifying. Also, there are so many strange places and rooms for you to find and youll discover something new every time you play. Just don’t waste your time with multiplayer because there’s hardly ever anyone there.
the random dark hallways are the scariest bit of the game because you don’t know if something could jump out of nowhere 😭😭 overall 10/10 best 2 dollars spent icl

developer_response.body: It keeps you on your toes! Thanks for giving us a 5 star review, our team are happy that you've enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description: So first no monsters and no multiplayer pls give money back
IT would be perfect if there's a game saving system so i dont have to start all over again every time(but i guess that;s the point)
Its very big and scary game to play, It made me feel different, like any other game did, It would be better with more places to explore

Got high asl, played the game blind. I thought I was tripping this game felt like a bad acid trip in vr! Endless yellow hell, straight panic inducing. Anxiety spiked like crazy. The sounds drove me insane!! Best horror game I’ve ever played. This scared me soo bad. Best 2 dollars spent on a game. Don’t play sober.
Its not worth it give it BACK!!!
What makes the game so scary is how dark it is and how quiet it is it would be awesome if they added flash lights 😭

The whole vibe to it has my spine shivering in each section that I go too. Some of the places  made me a bit queasy though, so warning to anyone with motion sickness, or any other vr sickness that you may have. The darkest hallways I expected to have some sort of monster in it but when there wasn't it added to the unexpecticity of the game. Out of all the weird, and paranormal activity that was crafted into this game, in my personal opinion that dang chair with the smiley face in black ink was the most unnerving. The reason for that was because it moved when you had looked away, and it gave you the idea that it would attack or something of the sort. Then out of nowhere in the yellow room section it just appeared out of the blue, giving me more chills. Amazing game, a must buy!

developer_response.body: Great to hear you've enjoyed all the secrets of our Backrooms! There's so much more to explore...

reviews.review_title: The best I've seen as a Backrooms Game
reviews.review_description: Highly recommend, it felt like I was actually getting lost and finding new areas that I've never seen before in only a couple playthroughs. Very eerie and creepy vibe and nice level design I would love for this to get updates
Better than noclip and you rarely get jumpscared which makes it more unnerving
I loved this in Banter, and love it standalone. This can give so many hours of exploration to anyone, I can highly recommend it if you like these kind of games. Love it!

I think this captures the backrooms atmosphere very well. It looks pretty good, but maybe slightly less impressive in the larger areas, but there's lots of variety in the locations.
Sometimes crouching down to enter tunnels gets a bit glitchy, and there are a few too many stairs that lead to dead ends. Also, 
I'd like there to be at least one entity that actually poses a genuine threat, as that would really up the anxiety stakes.
But I enjoy wandering around for half an hour or so, and it is unsettling, and definitely worth £1.99 of anyone's money.
Hope the developers continue to update this, it's something I'll keep dipping into to give me the chills.

For the first few minutes I was still wondering what all the fuss about "Liminal Space" was. It just felt like wandering around the first test of a game before gameplay elements were added. I have another liminal space game and while it was interesting, it wasn't creepy or compelling enough to play more than a couple of times.

But after a few minutes in this game I started to feel a bit unsettled. I think if I stayed long enough I'd start to get pretty anxious.  If I was there in real life I'd end up in a full blown panic.

It's a brilliant use of VR.

Unfortunately I had to leave early because something's really off about the locomotion.  I've been playing full locomotion VR games for almost ten years now and it's very difficult for me to get motion sickness, but this game started to make me feel a little sick after less than fifteen minutes.

I think it's because the stick is too sensitive. It's difficult to simply walk forward without wobbling left and right. The slightest movement of the stick throws you off course. Note that I turn with my feet, but I don't think that's the issue.

I had to knock a star off for this because I'd love to stay in the experience long enough to get seriously creeped out, but the locomotion system is preventing that.

I'm going to play it for a few minutes a day to see if I can build up a tolerance for the wobbly movement.

One other aspect I don't like is the cartoonish avatar with large hands and feet. It feels very out of place in this environment.  I'd love it if they added an option to shut off the full body avatar and just have floating hands, normal sized human hands if possible.

Update:

After playing some more I found an area where the scale changed dramatically. It was cool in an Alice in Wonderland sort of way. What wasn't cool is that the movement system totally failed here and it was extremely difficult to navigate. Crouching down in real life had very little effect and even while using the right stick to crouch down as low as possible it was nearly impossible to get through the doors. I had to quit the game at this point. I wonder if the game was tested with natural turning and crouching?

It's frustrating because the variety of areas really makes me want to keep exploring until the battery runs out but the technical issues are preventing that.

For now I can't give it more than three stars.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to try it, and for the detailed review. I think we need to tweak the character controller and it sounds like a better dead-zone is also essential. 

We will get these issues fixed asap and get a new update out - keep an eye out for that. 

Thank you again.

reviews.review_description: This game has all of the Kane Pixels locations and motifs. The graphics are quest2 but can forgive for the complexity and size of the levels. Was super creeped out most of the time but the feeling wears off when you realize there are no dangers or monsters :(  five stars when roaming enemy NPC is added and terrible avatar ( clown shoes?) is deleted or replaced by a more realistic asset. Keep polishing this and you could charge 10-15 bucks, easily.
